section_title: When a thing which is commonly the subject of sale is lost, if the owner
content:
169. When a thing which is commonly the subject of sale is lost, if the owner
of thing
cannot with reasonable diligence be found, or if he refuses, upon demand, to
commonly
on sale may pay the lawful charges of the finder, the finder may sell it–
sell it
(1) when the thing is in danger of perishing or of losing the greater part of its
value, or,
(2) when the lawful charges of the finder, in respect of the thing found,
amount to two-thirds of its value.
Bailee's
